purpose: Shadow previews are read-only references to another project's capability metadata or UI snapshot — never an implied live service, entitlement, or billing grant.
shadow_preview_definition: A static copy, screenshot, excerpt, or mirrored JSON/doc slice that documents what exists elsewhere, under explicit source labeling.
example_label_template: Shadow preview from {{source_project}} v{{version}} (freshness: {{iso_date}}). Read-only reference. Not a live service. Pricing and access remain owned by {{pricing_owner}} until a formal bundle charter exists.
rules: Shadow previews must not imply that the hosting project ships or bills for the source capability.
Freshness or version must be visible next to the preview.
Live execution, shared memory, iframe embeds, or API bridges require charter plus 14 DRP/DOP gate — not satisfied by a shadow preview.
Operators may remove or stale-mark previews when the source project revokes reference permission.
